light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing conditions on the Toccoa River are fair to good with steady flow of 230 cfs and gage height at 1.21 ft. Recent stocking supports use of egg patterns and attractor nymphs for productive outings.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning has the best fishing window due to moderate sky cover averaging 39%, high humidity near 96%, low rain probability, and mild air temperatures around 69.5°F, creating favorable conditions for trout activity and feeding.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current water flow is 230 cfs with a gage height of 1.21 ft. Water temperature is unavailable and turbidity data is not provided.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current sky cover averages 32.62% with pressure at 30.04 inHg showing no significant trend. Light wind averages 2.4 mph and there is minimal rain expected with a 44% chance of 0.01 inches.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely to hold in runs, seams, and near structure, favoring comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover. A balanced nymph approach with clean drifts using egg patterns, squirmy worms, and Duracell attractors will be effective. Presentation should emphasize smaller natural patterns and stocked trout attractors where appropriate.
